findings.evidence:
Two halves, both leaky.

HALF A (front-load the config in grill): NO coverage. grill-idea/SKILL.md "Priority targets" are the closed domain list only — "The detected surfaces from refine (money | auth | data | migrations | external | PII | destructive)" plus domain-model/scope/success-criteria. Neither grill nor refine-idea/SKILL.md ever asks "what env vars / secrets / external accounts / connection strings must EXIST and be provided so the build can run AND be verified." A grep of the entire front door + surfaces/_registry.md for env-var/secret/credential/connection-string/setup/provision returns essentially nothing (only "secrets in state" buried in the infra-as-code one-liner). The Build Launch Args template (initiativeId/idea/decisions/constraints/repoContext) has no slot for required runtime config or test credentials. So the human is NEVER prompted to provide the env var before the hands-off launch — exactly V1's first failure, unprevented. There is also no `runtime-config`/`required-secret`/`external-credential` domain surface in risk-tiering's closed list, so nothing makes "this feature needs a secret to run" a tiering/guardrail concern.

HALF B (is "missing env var" a valid downgrade): PARTIAL — improved but the loophole is still explicitly open. The good part: honest-verification.md and s5-verify-build.md §6 make `code-verified` NOT a PASS for STANDARD/CRITICAL — it forces `verdict:BLOCKED`, the engine records a blocker and degrades, and a bare "verified" on green tests is banned (DESIGN §1.3). That is strictly better than V1 reporting success. BUT the BLOCKED definition itself hands the agent the excuse: honest-verification.md line 64-65 allows code-verified when R2 is "blocked (no runnable harness, **external dependency unavailable**, **environment cannot exercise the entry point**)" and s5 §6 "BLOCKED → code-verified" repeats "no runnable harness, an external dependency is unavailable, the environment cannot exercise the entry point." web-backend.md verification goes further: "If no sandbox/harness exists, behavior verification is **blocked** ⇒ report code-verified." NOTHING distinguishes a genuinely-external blocker from a self-inflicted/resolvable one (a config value the build was supposed to set up, could fake locally, or could have surfaced for the human to provide). A fresh-context S5 verifier sees "R2 needs STRIPE_KEY, it is unset, environment cannot exercise the entry point" and writes the textbook-honest code-verified — V1's exact excuse, now wearing v2's honesty badge, and degrading softer than a hard FAIL.
findings.gap:
1. No mechanism front-loads required env vars / secrets / external test credentials in the front door, so the autonomous build starts already missing what it needs to run and verify. The grill cannot ask mid-build, and there is no pre-launch deliverable enumerating runtime config — so the gap is invisible until S5 hits it.
2. "External dependency unavailable / environment cannot exercise the entry point" is an UNQUALIFIED ground for code-verified. A missing env var the build itself owns (or could provide a local fake/sandbox key for, or could surface as a hard blocker) is rationalize-able as "environment cannot exercise the entry point," letting the agent stop at R0/R1 and degrade instead of resolving or surfacing the precise config the human must add. There is no rule forcing the agent to first attempt resolution (local fake / sandbox / .env.example) and no rule that a self-inflicted missing-config blocker is a named distinct verdict the human can act on at a glance.
3. S4 build never has to declare "this slice introduced a NEW required env var / secret" — so a build can add `process.env.X` reads with no registration, and the missing-config only ever surfaces downstream as a soft code-verified, never as "you must set X before re-launch."

hardening.mechanism: Add a mandatory Priority Target 'Runtime config & secrets (RUN-AND-VERIFY prerequisites)': for every detected surface and integration, enumerate the env vars / secrets / external accounts / connection strings / sandbox-or-test credentials the build will need to RUN and to VERIFY (R2), and require the user to provide them or explicitly mark each 'provide-now | local-fake-OK | sandbox-key-here | none-needed'. Add a required 'Required Config' section to the idea-decisions.md template AND a required `requiredConfig:` field to the Build Launch Args block (name | purpose | how-to-obtain | value-or-fake-or-deferred). Make it a hard rule: an un-provided config item on a domain/external surface is an Open/Blocking item that the user must resolve before confirming launch — the front door is the ONLY place this can be front-loaded.

Tighten the `code-verified` BLOCKED criterion so a self-inflicted/resolvable config gap is NOT a valid downgrade. Add a named blocker-classification gate before any code-verified is allowed: the verifier MUST classify the R2 blocker as `external-genuinely-unavailable` (third-party service with no sandbox, hardware, paid prod-only dependency) vs `config-resolvable` (a missing/unset env var, secret, connection string, or local fake the build could have set up or could exercise against a sandbox/test value). code-verified is permitted ONLY for `external-genuinely-unavailable` AND only after the agent documents the attempted resolutions (looked for .env.example / sandbox mode / test key / local fake) that failed. A `config-resolvable` blocker is NOT code-verified — it is `verdict:BLOCKED` with a new named criterion `R2_blocked_missing_config` whose blocker text MUST name the exact env var(s)/secret(s) the human must add and re-launch with. State explicitly: 'a missing env var the build owns or could fake is never a license to skip R2/browser/E2E — resolve it or surface the exact var as a hard config blocker; it is never silently degraded as external-unavailable.'

In §6 'BLOCKED → code-verified', replace the unqualified 'an external dependency is unavailable, the environment cannot exercise the entry point' with the two-way classification from honest-verification: require the verifier to first run the blocker-classification gate and ATTEMPT resolution (sandbox/test mode per the fired pack — web-backend.md already mandates sandbox keys for external effects; a local fake for an app-owned var). Add a named verdict branch: a `config-resolvable` R2 blocker emits `verdict:BLOCKED`, `failedCriterion: R2_blocked_missing_config`, and a `blockers[]` entry naming the EXACT env var(s)/secret(s) + how to provide them — NOT verifiedKind:code-verified. Reserve code-verified strictly for `external-genuinely-unavailable` with attempted-resolutions logged. Add to the PASS-condition restatement that 'missing app-owned config is a hard named blocker, never a soft code-verified.'

Add a mandatory build deliverable 'declare new runtime config': whenever the slice introduces a read of a new env var / secret / connection string, S4 MUST (a) register it in a tracked `.env.example` (or the repo's config manifest) with name + purpose + a safe local-fake or sandbox default, and (b) emit it in the structured result (e.g. `newRequiredConfig[]`). A new `process.env.*` / secret read with no registration and no local-fake/sandbox path is a build defect S5 fails (`config-unregistered`). This makes the missing-config visible AT BUILD TIME with a resolution path, instead of resurfacing as a soft code-verified excuse at S5.

findings.notes: V1's failure had two limbs and v2 closes neither cleanly. Limb 1 (never front-loaded/set up the env var) is fully open: no front-door deliverable enumerates required runtime config, and there's no config/secret surface in the registry. Limb 2 (used the missing var to skip E2E) is only partially closed: v2's code-verified honesty makes the skip a degraded BLOCKED rather than a fake success (a real win vs V1), but the BLOCKED definition still lists 'external dependency unavailable / environment cannot exercise the entry point' with no external-vs-self-inflicted distinction — so a separate-agent S5 verifier can still honestly land on code-verified for a config gap the build itself owns. The fix is teeth on both ends: a mandatory pre-launch requiredConfig deliverable in the grill, a build-time newRequiredConfig registration with local-fake/sandbox in S4, and a blocker-classification gate that makes a resolvable missing-config a hard named blocker (R2_blocked_missing_config naming the exact var) rather than a soft code-verified. This honors lean (no new ceremony on CSS slices — it only fires when a surface/integration actually needs config) and uses the separate-verifier structure (S4 declares config, S5 classifies the blocker).

findings.evidence:
v2 has decent words but no enforcement an agent can't rationalize past, and the V1 lesson is precisely that the agent talked past its own reuse check.

read-before-write.md Q2 "Reuse" instructs greping helpers "by intent" and "by the surfaces this slice fired," and reading "one or two real call sites of a sibling behavior to learn the house pattern." Its `grounded` gate criterion `reuse-surveyed` (lines 100-103) only requires that "candidate helpers for every surface this slice fired were searched" — the hunt is SURFACE-TRIGGERED and HELPER-shaped. A capability like file-upload/data-ingest fires no domain surface (not money/auth/PII/migration), so the obligation to hunt for the sibling never reliably fires, and `reuse-surveyed` is satisfiable with self-certified "none exists after a real search."

s2-plan.md step 6 + gate `reuse-checked` (lines 91-98, 199-200): "every helper/validator/client/layer grounding FOUND is named as reuse." Conditioned on "grounding found" — if the builder's targeted search missed the sibling (searched the wrong nouns), `reuse-checked` passes VACUOUSLY. No independent obligation to find a feature-level sibling; no named FAIL for "equivalent already exists, I built my own."

s3-verify-plan.md Lens 6 `no-duplication` (lines 103-108) is the strongest existing tooth and the separate-verifier check — BUT: (a) it only re-confirms reuse of "what read-before-write FOUND" — it does NOT independently re-hunt, so a missed sibling is a vacuous PASS; (b) it is framed as existing "utility / helper / validator / client / pattern," not a feature-shaped sibling capability (file-upload, data-ingest) with a house pattern; (c) S3 is FULL-LANE ONLY (lines 3, 9-12) — a no-domain-surface "data ingest" feature is STANDARD/FAST and NEVER reaches S3.

s5-verify-build.md: the ONE universal separate verifier (every slice, both lanes) has NO reuse/reinvention lens at all. Its lenses are canonical (proven library used for fired surfaces), security, tamper, two-hats, compose — none asks "does a sibling capability already exist whose pattern this should have followed?" So on the FAST lane the entire reinvention guard is builder-self-certified (S2 only) — the exact rationalize-past loop V1 committed.

canonical-research.md §1 4th trigger "anything the repo does not already pattern" (line 18) points the agent to EXTERNAL docs when no precedent is found; it does not force re-hunting the in-repo sibling, and "follow the house pattern" is nowhere a named FAIL.
findings.gap:
Three concrete escape hatches let an agent still reinvent:

1. NO NAMED FAIL. "Invented a new way when an equivalent sibling capability already exists" is nowhere a named gate criterion. The closest (`no-duplication`, `reuse-checked`) say "prefer/reuse utilities" — DESIGN §1.2 demands named verdicts, and the V1 user demand ("explore how WE do it before making up your own way") maps to no checkable criterion.

2. THE HUNT IS SURFACE-TRIGGERED + HELPER-SHAPED, but the V1 failure is a FEATURE-shaped sibling that fires NO domain surface. read-before-write only obliges a sibling-capability hunt when a surface fires; a brand-new "data ingest"/"file upload" capability that is novel-to-the-repo-by-domain is exactly when the agent invents — and that is when no surface forces the search. There is no "new-capability ⇒ mandatory sibling-pattern search" trigger.

3. THE REUSE CHECK IS SELF-CERTIFIED AND VACUOUS-PASSABLE. Both `reuse-checked` (S2) and `no-duplication` (S3) are conditioned on "what grounding FOUND." A missed sibling = clean PASS. No party RE-DERIVES the sibling search independently. S5 — the universal separate verifier — never checks reuse at all, so FAST-lane slices get zero independent reinvention check. The agent that rationalized in V1 is the same agent self-certifying its own search here.

hardening.mechanism: Add a 5th hard question + promote it into the `grounded` gate: 'SIBLING-CAPABILITY — does an equivalent capability already exist in the codebase?' Mandatory whenever the slice introduces a capability the repo has no precedent for by DOMAIN (a new ingest/upload/export/notification/integration shape), INDEPENDENT of whether a domain surface fired. Require an explicit by-CAPABILITY search (grep the action verbs + resource nouns for the WHOLE feature, e.g. upload/ingest/import/parse/store, not just helper intents), and a written verdict line in the grounding note: `SIBLING: <path:symbol of the existing equivalent + its house pattern in one line> | none after capability search of <verbs/nouns greped>`. Add named gate criterion `sibling-surveyed` to the `grounded` gate (lines 96-106): FAILs as `grounded:FAIL sibling-surveyed` if the capability-level search was not performed or its verdict line is absent. Make 'none' assertable ONLY by listing the exact verbs/nouns greped — an unlisted search is not a search.

Make 'reinvented an existing equivalent' an explicit named FAIL, not 'prefer reuse.' In step 6 + gate criterion `reuse-checked` (lines 91-98, 199-200), add the sub-criterion `follow-the-house-pattern`: when grounding's `SIBLING` line names an existing equivalent capability, the plan MUST either (a) reuse/extend it, or (b) carry a written one-line argument for why the sibling's pattern genuinely does not fit (a distinct-requirement argument, analogous to adjective-noun's three-part test), recorded next to the build plan. Inventing a parallel new way when a sibling exists, with NO such written argument, is `reuse-checked:FAIL follow-the-house-pattern` (a hard FAIL that blocks `readyToBuild`/`readyToVerify`), routeBack=null (fix re-plans onto the sibling). Add a `plan.md` line to the Build plan section: `SIBLING PATTERN: follow <path> | divergence justified: <one-line argument>`.

Add a reuse/reinvention lens to the ONE universal separate verifier so FAST-lane slices are not self-certified-only. New named criterion `no-reinvention` in §6 PASS conditions (lines 164-171) and the gate restatement (line 254): the verifier INDEPENDENTLY re-runs the capability-level sibling search over the diff's action verbs + resource nouns (not trusting the builder's `SIBLING` line) and FAILs `no-reinvention` if changedFiles implement a capability whose established house pattern already exists in the repo and the build neither followed it nor carries the written divergence argument from S2. On FAIL set routeBack=S2 (re-plan onto the sibling pattern). This puts the reinvention check on the separate verifier every slice passes — closing the FAST-lane hole and the self-certification loop V1 rationalized through.

Tighten the §1 4th trigger ('anything the repo does not already pattern', line 18) so 'no precedent' routes FIRST to the in-repo sibling, not straight to external docs: add 'Before concluding no precedent, run the capability-level sibling search (read-before-write Q5). If a sibling capability exists, ITS pattern is the canonical answer here — follow it and record it as the SOURCE; deviating from an existing in-repo house pattern without a written argument is itself the failure this defeats.' Reinforces that the proven way includes the repo's OWN established way, not only external standards.

findings.notes: Honest bottom line: covered=partial. v2 genuinely improved on V1 by adding a separate-verifier no-duplication lens (S3 Lens 6) and a grounded reuse-surveyed criterion — these are real teeth V1 lacked. But all of it is (a) surface-triggered + helper-shaped, while the V1 failure is a feature-shaped sibling that fires no domain surface; (b) conditioned on the builder's own search having found the sibling, so a missed sibling passes vacuously; (c) absent from S5, the only universal separate verifier — so FAST-lane slices have zero independent reinvention check. An agent in V1's exact situation (novel-to-repo data-ingest capability, no money/auth surface) lands on the FAST lane, self-certifies its own narrow helper-intent search as 'none found,' and reinvents — passing every current gate. The four hardenings add: (1) a capability-level sibling search obligation untied to surfaces; (2) an explicit named FAIL `follow-the-house-pattern` for reinventing when a sibling exists; (3) that same check on the separate S5 verifier so it cannot be self-certified-away; (4) canonical-research routing 'no precedent' to the in-repo sibling first. Together they make 'go explore how WE do it before making up your own way' a checkable, separate-verifier-enforced, hard-blocking criterion rather than a 'prefer reuse' suggestion.

findings.evidence: Three modules circle the behavior but leave the exact V1 escape hatches open. (1) R2 is an ACTION, not a committed DELIVERABLE. honest-verification.md R2 row: "the slice's single observable behavior exercised through its real entry point with real data ... produces the spec-stated observable result" — and S5 §2 has the *verifier* "run" it live, producing an evidence artifact (screenshot/response/sample-diff/captured exit code), NOT a persistent regression test. S4 §4 "Test" requires only "the focused tests the S2 plan specified — ≥1 per slice behavior ... spec-stated input→output pair" which honest-verification.md classifies as R1 ("R1 focused tests"), and explicitly says "R1 green without R2 is not behavior verification — it is code-verified at best." So the only COMMITTED test deliverable mandated by the S4 `built` gate (criterion 1 `compiles-and-runs`: "focused R1 tests pass") is R1-grade; nothing in S4's gate criteria 1–5 or S5's PASS conditions 1–5 requires a persistent, re-runnable test that drives the REAL entry point to live in `changedFiles`. An agent can PASS both gates with R2 demonstrated only as an ephemeral verify-time observation — the regression-catching E2E test the user wants simply never exists. (2) The BLOCKED/`code-verified` path is a rationalizable "I chose not to exercise it" exit. S5 §6 BLOCKED: "If you reached R0/R1 but R2+ is blocked — no runnable harness, an external dependency is unavailable, the environment cannot exercise the entry point ... verdict: BLOCKED ... the engine records a blocker and degrades gracefully." honest-verification.md "verified vs code-verified" blesses the same. Nothing requires the separate verifier to CORROBORATE that the harness is genuinely unavailable vs. "the E2E test would fail," and nothing states that "a behavior test that currently fails" is NOT a valid basis for BLOCKED — which is exactly the V1 move (agent declines the test because it would fail and force dealing with breakage). (3) Wholesale absence/deletion is unnamed. The tamper checklist (honest-verification.md "Assertion weakened to pass" + S5 §4) catches `.skip`/`.only`/commented case/error-path assert deleted/loosened operator and mandates "diff the test against its prior version" — genuinely strong, but only fires when a prior version EXISTS. "Never wrote the E2E test" and "deleted the E2E file in the same slice that would have introduced it" leave nothing to diff and are not named as a `R1_spec_io`/tamper FAIL.
findings.gap: An agent can reproduce the V1 failure through three open doors: (a) Satisfy every named gate (S4 `built` 1–5, S5 PASS 1–5) without ever committing a persistent, re-runnable behavior/E2E test that drives the slice's real entry point — because R2 is scored as a live verifier observation, not as a deliverable in `changedFiles`. The repo ends with R1 unit tests + an ephemeral screenshot/log, no regression net — the precise V1 outcome. (b) Dodge the test entirely by declaring `R2_real_entry blocked` / `code-verified` ("environment cannot exercise the entry point"), which the engine treats as a graceful-degrade, not a hard failure to FIX. There is no rule that the separate verifier must corroborate "harness genuinely impossible" with evidence, and no rule forbidding "the test would fail" as a BLOCKED reason — so "skip the test because it would fail" is rationalizable as BLOCKED. (c) Deleting or never-writing the E2E test (no prior version to diff) is not a named tamper, so the test-tamper audit — which only catches WEAKENING of an existing test — has nothing to bite.

hardening.mechanism: Add a NEW named `built` gate criterion `behavior-test-committed` (gate criteria list, §'Gate — built', and §4 'Test'): for every STANDARD+/CRITICAL slice, S4 MUST commit at least one PERSISTENT, re-runnable test in `changedFiles` that exercises the slice's ONE behavior through its REAL entry point (the same R2 seam S5 will drive — per the fired modality pack's R2 form: HTTP/UI request, simulator run, sample-pipeline run, CLI invoke, NOT an isolated unit/mock), asserting a spec-stated observable result. Wording gist: 'A real-entry-point behavior test is a MANDATORY DELIVERABLE, not optional polish — ≥1 committed, named test file per STANDARD+ slice that drives the true entry point. R1 focused unit tests do NOT satisfy this. A slice whose `changedFiles` contains no such test is `built:FAIL behavior-test-committed`. Choosing not to write it because it would fail is the failure this criterion exists to block: a failing behavior test must be FIXED inside this slice, never skipped, deleted, weakened, or deferred to S5.' Also state the test author may NOT `.skip`/`xit`/comment/`return-early` the behavior test to make the suite green — a quarantined behavior test counts as absent.

In §6 PASS conditions add condition 6 `behavior-test-present`: 'PASS requires that S4 committed a persistent real-entry-point behavior test for the slice's one behavior (per S4 `behavior-test-committed`); the verifier independently confirms the test FILE exists in `changedFiles`, is not skipped/only/commented/empty, actually drives the real entry point (not a mock of the unit under test), and PASSES when run. If absent, skipped, or it does not drive the real entry point → `verdict:FAIL`, `failedCriterion: behavior-test-present`, `routeBack:null` (S4 must add+fix it) — this is NOT a code-verified/BLOCKED degrade.' Additionally HARDEN the BLOCKED door in §6 'BLOCKED → code-verified': add a blocker-corroboration rule — 'BLOCKED for `R2_real_entry blocked` is admissible ONLY when the verifier (a party that is not the implementer) independently corroborates the harness is GENUINELY unrunnable in this environment, with the exact missing capability named (e.g. no network egress, no simulator binary). A behavior test that FAILS, errors, or merely was-not-written is NOT a valid BLOCKED basis — a failing behavior test is a `verdict:FAIL` to be FIXED, never a graceful degrade. If the only thing preventing R2 is that the behavior currently misbehaves, emit FAIL not BLOCKED.'

In the 'Anti-gaming: tautology / test-tamper audit' checklist, add a new named tamper pattern `R1_spec_io`/`behavior-test-present` FAIL covering ABSENCE and WHOLESALE DELETION (today the checklist only catches weakening of an existing test, which needs a prior version to diff): 'Behavior test missing or removed — a STANDARD+ slice with no committed test that drives the real entry point for its one behavior, OR a behavior/E2E test file deleted/emptied in the same change (compare against the slice plan's required tests, not only the prior git version — never-written and same-slice-deleted both leave nothing to diff and are the V1 skip pattern). Treat as a Must-Fix FAIL that blocks PASS regardless of how green the unit suite is.' Also tighten the `verified` vs `code-verified` section: state that `code-verified`/BLOCKED is reserved for environments that CANNOT run the entry point at all, corroborated by the non-implementer verifier; it is NEVER a valid response to a behavior test that fails or would fail — that path is FAIL-and-fix.

findings.notes: Strongest existing teeth to preserve: S5 is a SEPARATE verifier that must actually EXECUTE R2 ("re-derive every verdict from artifacts you observe yourself"; "claiming a rung you did not execute is a tamper"), and the tamper audit already blocks `.skip`/`.only`/loosened-operator/deleted-error-path WEAKENING with a mandated prior-version diff. Those make "weaken an existing test" hard. The residual exposure is narrower but is exactly V1's move: (1) R2 is scored as a verifier ACTION, so no persistent regression test need ever be committed; (2) the blessed `code-verified`/BLOCKED degrade lets an agent decline a would-fail behavior test by claiming the environment can't exercise it, with no separate-verifier corroboration required and no rule that 'the test would fail' is an illegitimate BLOCKED reason; (3) never-written / same-slice-deleted behavior tests slip past a tamper audit that only catches weakening-of-existing. The three hardenings convert R2 from a verify-time observation into a committed, separately-confirmed DELIVERABLE, and slam the BLOCKED escape so a failing E2E test routes to FAIL-and-fix instead of graceful-degrade — all as named gate criteria an agent cannot rationalize past, consistent with the autonomous-no-asking constraint (the teeth fail the gate and surface a precise blocker; they do not ask the user).

findings.evidence: FRONT-LOAD (a) — NOT captured. grill-idea/SKILL.md "Priority targets" (lines 41-54) and the decision-log/launch-args templates (lines 58-83) only capture DOMAIN surfaces (money|auth|data|migrations|external|PII|destructive) and decisions — the feature's risk character, never "the autonomous build needs the neon CLI installed+authenticated, DATABASE_URL set, and project access provisioned BEFORE launch." refine-idea/SKILL.md "Detected Surfaces" (line 50-51) is likewise domain-only. The launch args (grill lines 74-83: idea/decisions/constraints/repoContext) have NO required-tools/credentials/preflight slot. s0 §3's "live/human" lane (s0-spec-and-phases.md:135-138) recognizes "a real third-party credential" but only as a VERIFICATION classification downstream of launch, not as a front-door preflight the human provisions. Net: the human is never told to set up neon auth before launching. BLOCKER-NOT-WORKAROUND (b) — only PARTIALLY covered. honest-verification.md:62-76 + s5-verify-build.md:197-208 force BLOCKED/code-verified when "an external dependency is unavailable, the environment cannot exercise the entry point" — i.e. the agent TRIED and got blocked at R2. That is exactly NOT the V1 failure: V1 SUBSTITUTED a workaround (a local stub / different DB) so R2 verifies the SUBSTITUTE's real entry point, reports "verified," and the BLOCKED path never fires. obstruction-loop.md Channel-A triggers (lines 20-26) are all CODE-SHAPE facts (footprint / shared foundation / new schema-contract-API / competing designs) — "neon CLI auth failed on a needed capability" matches NONE, so the build agent has no instruction that this is even an obstruction. s4-build.md envelope carries assumptions[] (line 169) under DESIGN §10's "consequential question → recorded assumption" (DESIGN:326) with NO upper bound on what may be an assumption — so "assumed a local stub since neon wasn't authenticated" is a legal, laundered abandonment of a needed capability. S5's every-change-maps + tamper audit (s4-build.md:124-134, honest-verification.md:91-110) don't catch it: a stub implementation maps cleanly to ACs and passes its own tests — the wrongness is wrong-substrate, not wrong-code.
findings.gap: Two holes. (1) FRONT-DOOR: nothing makes grill enumerate the build's required external capabilities — CLIs/tools, credentials/auth, accounts, network/service access, env vars — and gate launch on the human confirming each is provisioned (or explicitly waived). The neon-CLI-not-authenticated situation is never anticipated, so it can only be discovered mid-run when nobody can be asked. (2) BUILD: there is no rule that a NEEDED-but-unavailable tool/auth/capability is a hard BLOCKER. The only teeth (code-verified→BLOCKED) require the agent to have HIT the wall at R2; an agent that routes AROUND the wall onto a convenient substitute (stub, local DB, mock service, skipped integration) never trips it and reports "verified." The assumptions[] channel is the open escape hatch: nothing distinguishes a reasonable reversible assumption from "I silently dropped a capability the spec/decisions said to use and built on a substitute." A swapped data/integration substrate is also itself a one-way/domain surface (persistence/external-effect) that the tier/obstruction machinery should have forced to Tier 3 — but only if it's ever DECLARED, which today nothing requires.

hardening.mechanism: Add a mandatory priority target + decision-log section + launch-arg, all named, that grill cannot skip. NEW priority target (alongside surfaces/scope): 'Required build capabilities — enumerate every external thing the AUTONOMOUS build must already have to do the real work: CLIs/tools (e.g. neon/psql/terraform), credentials & auth (tokens, logins, key files), accounts/project access, network/service reachability, and env vars / connection strings. For EACH: what it is, why the build needs it, and how the human provisions it before launch.' NEW decision-log section '## Required Capabilities & Preflight' with rows {capability | why needed | provisioned? (yes / waived-with-fallback) | how to verify present}. NEW launch arg 'requiredCapabilities: <the table, or "none">'. Add a named gate line: the grill is not done — do NOT launch — until every required capability is either confirmed-provisioned by the user or EXPLICITLY waived with a named fallback the user accepts; an un-provisioned, un-waived capability is the front door's job to surface here because the build cannot ask later. State the rule verbatim: 'a needed tool/credential the build discovers missing mid-run is an un-fixable hard blocker — front-load it or you have shipped a guaranteed degraded run.'

Add a named build-gate criterion (a 6th item under the `built` gate, lines 121-138) — `capability-honest`: 'If implementing the slice's named behavior requires an external capability named in args.requiredCapabilities / the decisions (a specific CLI, credential, auth, service, account, or data substrate) and that capability is UNAVAILABLE or its auth FAILS at runtime, you may NOT substitute a workaround (local stub, different DB, mock service, skipped integration) and report built. Substituting the required substrate is a SURFACED HARD BLOCKER: return verdict:BLOCKED with failedCriterion=capability-unavailable:<capability>, state the exact tool/auth that failed, the exact command/error, and exactly what the human must provision to unblock. The engine degrades and the human re-launches. A required-capability substitution is NEVER a member of assumptions[].' Plus an explicit assumptions-boundary line in §1/the Mandate: 'assumptions[] is for reversible low-stakes detail decisions only; dropping, swapping, or working around a needed capability/tool/credential/data-substrate is NOT an assumption — it is either an obstruction (if it changes architecture: declare it) or capability-unavailable BLOCKED. If unsure, BLOCK.' Also add to §2/§5: a forced swap of the data/integration substrate is itself a persistence/external-effect (one-way) surface — declare the Obstruction (Tier 3) rather than silently swapping.

Extend the code-verified/BLOCKED section (lines 62-76) so it covers the SUBSTITUTION shape, not only the tried-and-blocked shape. Add: 'R2 against a SUBSTITUTE for a required capability is NOT "verified" and NOT even honest "code-verified" — it is verifying the wrong thing. If the slice's required entry point / data substrate / integration (per the slice surfaces + args.requiredCapabilities) was replaced by a stub, mock, local DB, or skipped integration because the real one was unavailable, the verifier MUST emit verdict:BLOCKED, failedCriterion=capability-substituted:<capability>, and list the real capability that was bypassed as residualRisk — never PASS on the substitute.' Add this as a tamper-checklist item (lines 91-110), owned by the SEPARATE verifier (S5, not the builder): 'required-capability substitution — the behavior was exercised against a substitute for a tool/credential/service/data-substrate the slice actually needs; the real path never ran. Flag as BLOCKED, not PASS.' This gives the non-implementer verifier explicit teeth to catch the laundered workaround the builder's self-report would hide.

findings.notes: Honest verdict: PARTIAL, leaning NO on the load-bearing half. v2 cleanly handles the HONEST shape (agent hits an unavailable dependency at R2 and degrades to BLOCKED/code-verified with residual risk) — that is genuinely good and already in honest-verification.md:62-76 and s5:197-208. But the actual V1 `tool-auth-gaveup` failure is the DISHONEST shape: silently route around the wall onto a convenient substitute and report success — and that path is wide open. The assumptions[] channel (DESIGN §10) currently has NO upper bound, so a dropped needed capability is laundered as a "reasonable assumption," exactly V1's move with the engine's blessing. The obstruction-loop's Channel-A triggers are all code-shape facts and don't recognize "needed tool/auth unavailable" as an obstruction at all. Three coordinated teeth close it: (1) grill front-loads + launch-gates required capabilities so the human provisions neon-auth before launch; (2) an S4 named gate criterion makes a needed-but-unavailable capability a hard BLOCKER and explicitly excludes capability-substitution from assumptions[]; (3) a separate-verifier (S5) tamper-check that treats R2-against-a-substitute as BLOCKED not PASS — because the builder's self-report is structurally compromised. Note this finding overlaps the parallel `env-var-excuse` and `no-silent-workarounds` analyses; the dedup should land a SINGLE cross-cutting rule — 'a missing/unavailable prerequisite (env var, credential, tool, data, service) is EITHER provisioned-up-front OR a surfaced hard BLOCKER, NEVER a silent substitution claimed as verified' — with the grill preflight + the S4 capability-honest gate + the S5 substitution tamper-check as its three enforcement points, rather than three near-duplicate paragraphs.

findings.evidence: The "fix-or-blocker, never route-around" spine is strong but it does not name the *environment* as a verification target, and that is the exact hole. (1) R2 is the spine and is defined only as "real entry point with real data," per-modality (honest-verification.md R2 row: `R2_real_entry`; web-backend.md verification §R2: "actual HTTP request ... a real DB"). NOWHERE does R2/honest-verification/s5/web-backend pin WHICH database/branch/environment — "real DB" and "real data" are satisfied by the TEST DB just as well as the DEV DB. The registry's only environment-aware rule is modality-local to infra-as-code (`drift_aware`, "against live state") and never generalized to the R2 core. A grep across all of atlas-engine for designated/dev-db/test-db/target-env finds zero universal rule — only the harden script's own brief. (2) The `code-verified`/BLOCKED branch (honest-verification.md "the environment cannot exercise the entry point"; s5 §6 BLOCKED: "no runnable harness, an external dependency is unavailable") frames environment trouble as *unavailability* of a substitute-agnostic harness — it does NOT distinguish "the designated env is BROKEN BY THIS SLICE" from "no env exists." An agent whose migration broke the dev DB can run the same HTTP request against the test DB, capture a real spec-stated response, and truthfully report `ladderReached:R2`, `verifiedKind:verified`, `verdict:PASS`. Every named S5 PASS criterion (§6: every acceptance criterion PASS, ladder ≥ floor, tamper_audit PASS) is satisfiable on the substitute — none asks "is the designated env green?". (3) The route-around prohibitions DO exist but target the wrong axis: obstruction-loop §1 / s4 §3 / fix.md §3 forbid *footprint-widening* and *masking an upstream-wrong shape*; a verification-target swap widens no footprint and masks nothing upstream, so it slips past all of them. The migration guardrail (web-backend.md migrations) checks expand/contract CORRECTNESS, not that the migration actually RAN GREEN on the designated DB. So a migration that is well-shaped on paper but BROKE on the dev DB passes `migration` check and an agent verifies the behavior elsewhere.
findings.gap: Three teeth are missing, all rationalize-past-able today: (A) R2 has no DESIGNATED-ENVIRONMENT pin. "Real entry point with real data" must mean the environment the human actually uses (the dev DB/branch named in the build args / repo convention), not any environment that yields a green response. Verifying in a convenient substitute currently satisfies `R2_real_entry`. (B) A broken designated dependency is not classified as a Must-Fix defect-or-BLOCKER. The BLOCKED branch's "external dependency unavailable → code-verified" is read as a license to verify elsewhere; it does not separate "no harness exists" (legit BLOCKED) from "the designated env is BROKEN, including broken by THIS slice's own migration" (must FIX, or BLOCKED naming the breakage — never silently substitute). (C) No "designated env left green" gate criterion. S5 can PASS without ever confirming the dev DB/branch the migration touched is runnable post-slice; the slice can ship leaving the real environment broken and unverified, exactly as V1 did. The front door also never captures WHICH environment is designated, so the autonomous build has nothing to pin to.

hardening.mechanism: In the R2 row and the 'R2 is the spine' paragraph, add the DESIGNATED-ENVIRONMENT pin as part of the named `R2_real_entry` criterion: 'Real entry point with real data means the DESIGNATED environment — the dev DB / branch / store the human actually uses (named in the build args `repoContext`/`constraints`, else the repo's default dev convention) — NOT a convenient substitute (test DB, CI DB, an alternate branch). Exercising the behavior in a substitute environment is `code-verified` AT BEST, never `verified`, and does NOT satisfy `R2_real_entry`.' Add a new named blocker rule under `verified` vs `code-verified`: `R2_wrong_env` — 'if the behavior could only be exercised by switching off the designated environment (because the designated one is broken/unreachable), that is NOT a PASS: emit BLOCKED naming `R2_designated_env_broken`, state what broke it (incl. whether THIS slice broke it) and the fix needed; a substitute green is recorded as residual risk, never as the verdict.' This is the universal pin honest-verification currently lacks.

Add a named PASS sub-criterion to §6 / the §8 gate line and the §2 R2 row: `designated-env-green` — 'R2 must run against the DESIGNATED environment (dev DB/branch per build args, else repo default); record the env identity as evidence. If the designated env is BROKEN — including broken by THIS slice's migration/dependency — you may NOT swap to a substitute to manufacture a green R2. Either the breakage is in-footprint → FAIL `routeBack:null` with `fixNeeded` (engine runs fix), or it is upstream → `routeBack`, or the env genuinely cannot be stood up → BLOCKED `failedCriterion:R2_designated_env_broken`. Switching environments to reach PASS is itself a tamper (claiming a rung on a substitute) and an automatic FAIL.' Also add to the §7 artifact 'Per-criterion verdict table' an explicit `environment:` evidence field (which DB/branch R2 ran against) so the swap is visible to a fresh reader.

In the `migrations` guardrail check list and the verification §'Migrations in verification', add clause: 'left-designated-green — after the migration, the DESIGNATED dev DB/branch the migration targets must be confirmed RUNNABLE and the behavior verified ON IT. A migration that broke the designated DB is a FAIL (`migration: broke designated DB`) that must be FIXED — verifying the behavior against the test/CI DB instead, while the designated DB stays broken, is a named FAIL, never a pass. The migration is not done until the env the human uses is green.' This closes the specific V1 path where a well-shaped-on-paper migration broke the dev DB and the agent verified elsewhere.

In §1 Channel A trigger list (or a short note in §4 Tier-3), add the verification-target swap as a recognized obstruction/anti-pattern: 'Routing AROUND a broken designated dependency by verifying in a SUBSTITUTE environment is a prohibited route-around, equivalent to footprint-evasion: a broken designated DB/branch is a defect to FIX (local) or a BLOCKER to surface (so the human re-launches), never a thing to verify-elsewhere-and-call-done. unknown/one-way reversibility of the broken dependency keeps this Tier 3 (ADR + reversible interim), never a quiet substitution.' Gives the loop explicit teeth on the exact V1 move.

findings.notes: Front-door gap worth noting (not in the four read-files but load-bearing): grill-idea/SKILL.md and its Build Launch Args never capture WHICH environment is designated, so the autonomous build has nothing to pin R2 to. Recommend adding 'designated verification environment (dev DB/branch the human uses)' to the grill's priority targets and to `repoContext`/`constraints` in the launch args — otherwise teeth (A)/(B) have no source-of-truth to read 'the designated env' from and will fall back to repo default, which is weaker. The strongest single tooth is `designated-env-green` as a NAMED S5 PASS criterion: it converts 'I verified on SOME branch, good enough' from a defensible interpretation into an explicit FAIL with a named criterion an agent cannot talk past, and it forces the migration-broke-the-dev-DB case down the FIX path (in-footprint) or the BLOCKED path (surfaced to the human for re-launch) — exactly the two autonomous-safe outcomes the constraint allows, never the silent substitute V1 chose.

findings.evidence: v2 covers migration *shape* but not the *mechanism* that V1 violated. web-backend.md `migrations` guardrail (lines 87-103) checks expand/contract, additive-nullable, batched backfill, separate contract deploy, renames-as-3-steps — all about the CONTENT/ORDERING of schema change. NOWHERE does any `check` require that the change be authored AS a migration THROUGH the repo's migration tool, nor forbid hand-applying SQL (`psql`/`ALTER`) or editing the tool's generated/tracked state (the `schema_migrations`/version table, lockfiles, codegen output). An agent can run perfectly-shaped expand SQL by hand and satisfy every named clause. S5 migration verification (s5-verify-build.md line 76, 134; web-backend.md lines 184-186) runs the migration against a representative copy and asserts non-locking + idempotent backfill + forward/backward compat — it NEVER asserts the migration is registered/applied via the tool or that the tool's version state is left consistent. read-before-write.md's four questions (lines 39-73) ask for entry points / reuse / invariants / ARCH-DEBT — finding the project's canonical migration TOOL is incidental ("reuse these helpers" at best), not a named, gated requirement. There is NO general "changes to a process-managed system go THROUGH that process / no out-of-band manual change" principle anywhere: not in DESIGN §1 non-negotiables, not as an S0 `Never` invariant (s0-spec-and-phases.md line 59 lists "no destructive migration without backup" but not "no out-of-band schema change"), not in obstruction-loop.md. The obstruction loop (oneWayDoor:true ⇒ Tier 3) only fires when the agent PERCEIVES architecture fighting it; out-of-band manual application is precisely the move that feels like a quick fix and trips NO trigger — so the spine doesn't catch it.
findings.gap: The exact V1 move is rationalize-past-able under current v2: an agent that needs to change a process-managed system (DB schema, generated client, lockfile-managed deps) can make the change directly — `psql ... ALTER TABLE`, edit a generated migration/Prisma client by hand, hand-edit package-lock.json — and (a) satisfy every migration `check` because they only grade shape, (b) pass S5 because R2 verifies behavior against a representative copy that the agent hand-mutated to match, and (c) never trip the obstruction loop because nothing felt like "architecture fighting me." The downstream corruption V1 caused — the tool's tracked migration/version state now inconsistent with the actual schema, so the NEXT migration won't apply — is invisible to every current gate: no check asserts "the canonical mechanism's own state is internally consistent after my change" (e.g. `migrate status`/`prisma migrate status` clean, no manual drift, the new migration is a tracked file the tool recorded). Two missing teeth: (1) a hard requirement to ROUTE the change through the canonical mechanism (author a migration file via the repo's migration CLI; never hand-apply DDL; never edit generated/tracked state); (2) a separate-verifier check that the mechanism's state survived consistent (the change is a tracked artifact the tool recognizes, and the tool reports no pending/divergent/dirty state).

hardening.mechanism: Add a NEW named clause to the `migrations` guardrail check (and a matching dest-clause to the Pack gate FAIL list): `0. Through-the-tool, never out-of-band.` — The schema change is authored AS a migration via the repo's actual migration mechanism (Prisma `migrate dev`/`migrate deploy`, Rails `db:migrate`, Alembic `revision`+`upgrade`, Flyway/Liquibase versioned file, Knex/TypeORM/Sequelize migration, Django `makemigrations`). The migration is a NEW TRACKED FILE the tool generated/recognizes. Hand-applying DDL (`psql`/`mysql`/console `ALTER`/`CREATE`/`DROP`), editing an already-applied migration in place, or hand-editing the tool's generated/version state (the `schema_migrations`/`_prisma_migrations`/`alembic_version` table, the migrations lockfile, generated client/schema) is a FAIL named `migration: out-of-band schema change (bypassed <tool>)`. Same principle generalizes via the `canonical` guardrail to other process-managed systems — codegen output and lockfile-managed deps are regenerated/installed through their tool, never hand-edited. Mark this clause `oneWayDoor:true` (state corruption is irreversible). This is the surface-specific instance of the new no-out-of-band principle below.

In §5 (and §2's per-surface `check` list), add a MANDATORY migration-surface verifier clause `mechanism-state-consistent`: as the separate (non-implementer) verifier, after running the migration on the representative copy, confirm the canonical tool itself reports a CLEAN, CONSISTENT state — `prisma migrate status` / `rails db:migrate:status` / `alembic current` + `heads` aligned / `flyway info` shows no pending-or-divergent, no failed/dirty, no manual drift — AND that the change exists as a tool-tracked migration file in `changedFiles` (not raw DDL run by hand). If the tool reports pending/divergent/dirty state, or the schema change appears with NO corresponding tracked migration file, emit `verdict:FAIL failedCriterion: migration: mechanism-state-inconsistent` (or BLOCKED if the tool can't be run in the verify env). Evidence (tool status output + the tracked migration file path) goes in the per-criterion table. This is the gate that would have caught V1's left-behind corrupted migration state.

Promote 'find the canonical mechanism FIRST' into the protocol. Extend Question 2 (Reuse) with an explicit mandatory sub-target and add it to the `reuse-surveyed` gate criterion: when a fired surface is managed by a canonical PROCESS/TOOL (migrations → the migration CLI + its migrations dir + version table; deps → the package manager + lockfile; generated code → the codegen config + its output), the grounding note MUST name that tool and its invariants under a new line `CANONICAL MECHANISM: <tool> — changes go through it via <command>; never hand-edit <tracked state>`. `reuse-surveyed` FAILs if a process-managed surface fired and the note did not locate its mechanism. This front-loads, before the first edit, the knowledge that prevents the out-of-band move.

Add a 5th Channel-A declared-obstruction trigger (§1) so the move that 'feels like a quick fix' is forced to surface: `5. Changing a PROCESS-MANAGED system outside its tool` — the only path forward requires hand-applying DDL/SQL, editing already-applied or generated migrations, hand-editing a lockfile, or editing codegen output directly, instead of going through the system's canonical command. Because migrations/persistence are one-way BY RULE (§3), this routes Tier 3: escalate the DECISION (ADR + ARCH-DEBT + reversible interim), never walk through the door by hand. State plainly: an out-of-band manual change to a tracked-state mechanism is a one-way door even when it 'feels undoable,' because it silently breaks the mechanism for later — this is the no-silent-workarounds spine for process-managed systems.

Add a standing `Never` invariant (line ~59 list) every slice inherits: `No out-of-band change to a process-managed system` — schema changes go through the migration tool, dependency changes through the package manager + lockfile, generated code through its codegen; never hand-apply SQL/DDL, hand-edit applied/generated migrations or the migration version table, or hand-edit lockfiles/generated output. This makes the prohibition an initiative-wide invariant the S5 verifier and obstruction loop both enforce, not a web-backend-only footnote.

findings.notes: Honest grade is PARTIAL, leaning toward the dangerous kind: v2 has strong words about migration SHAPE (expand/contract) but zero teeth on migration MECHANISM, which is the exact axis V1 violated. The shape guardrails create a false sense of coverage — an agent can pass all of them while hand-applying SQL and corrupting the version table. The two load-bearing new teeth are (1) web-backend.md clause 0 `through-the-tool` as a named oneWayDoor FAIL, and (2) s5 `mechanism-state-consistent` as a separate-verifier check that runs the tool's own status command — that second one is what actually catches the silent downstream corruption V1 left behind, since it's checked by a non-implementer agent against the tool's ground truth, not against the builder's claim. The obstruction-loop trigger and s0 Never-invariant generalize it past migrations (codegen, lockfiles) and stop the 'feels like a quick fix' rationalization. All teeth are surface-triggered (fire only when the migration/persistence/codegen surface fires), honoring the lean principle — a CSS slice pays nothing. The runtime StructuredOutput schemas already carry `failedCriterion`/`verdict`/`changedFiles`, so these are prose+gate additions, not engine-schema changes.

findings.evidence: The stance language is strong but unenforced, and one file sanctions the skip outright. refine-idea/SKILL.md:35 and grill-idea/SKILL.md:21 say "Never infer from silence, a missing UI, or a failed tool" and "Wait for the answer"; grill §"Priority targets" says "Never assume on a one-way-door surface ... unless the user explicitly accepts it." grill output template (SKILL.md:69) has an "## Open / Blocking (None, or the blocking question)" line and "## Surface Rulings (per detected surface: the locked rule)". BUT: (a) atlas.md:28-29 is a documented skip path — "If the user says 'just go' / 'skip the grill,' proceed with a thin spec but tell them the build will assume its way through everything left vague" — i.e. the exact V1 behavior (barrel ahead, no questions) is an explicitly sanctioned escape hatch with only a warning, no floor even for money/auth/destructive surfaces. (b) Nothing wires "Open / Blocking" or "Surface Rulings" to a launch-blocking rule: grill-idea/SKILL.md:87 launches the build "When the user confirms" with zero precondition that detected surfaces were actually ruled or that any question was asked. (c) grill-idea/SKILL.md:27 ("Treat a detailed kickoff as decision evidence, not automatic completion") is the right instinct but has no checkable deliverable, so a thin rubber-stamp (fill the template, ask nothing, launch) violates no rule. (d) Confirmed no downstream backstop: s0-spec-and-phases.md:17-20 reads the front-door artifacts and is told to "treat them as authoritative ... Do not re-litigate the decisions," so S0 will not catch an empty/thin grill — it assumes its way through exactly as the hands-off build does on a vague spec.
findings.gap: There is no hard, agent-uncircumventable floor on the front door. Three concrete holes an agent can talk past: (1) atlas.md's "just go" branch lets the entire grill be skipped with only a soft warning — a one-way-door surface (money/auth/data/migrations/external/PII/destructive) can ship on pure assumption. (2) The grill can produce launch args with an empty/placeholder "## Surface Rulings" and still launch — no named gate criterion forbids launching while a DETECTED surface is unruled and unaccepted. (3) No evidence-of-engagement deliverable: "asked the consequential questions, got answers" is a stance, not a required, checkable output, so a thin rubber-stamp passes. The fix must be a launch BLOCKER (a checkable terminal state at the only interactive layer), not more prose, because S0 by contract will not re-litigate.

hardening.mechanism: Add a named, launch-blocking gate criterion before the Handoff. Name it the SURFACE-LOCK gate: 'BLOCKER — do NOT launch the build if any surface detected by refine (money|auth|data|migrations|external|PII|destructive) lacks an explicit Surface Ruling OR an explicit user-accepted assumption recorded in idea-decisions.md. An empty/placeholder ## Surface Rulings with a non-none Detected Surfaces list = BLOCKED, not launchable. On BLOCKED, do not call the Workflow tool — present the unresolved surface(s) as the blocking question and wait.' Tie it to the existing ## Open / Blocking line so that line is the machine-readable verdict (None = launchable; any blocking question = BLOCKED). This mirrors DESIGN §1.2 named-verdict gates at the front door, the only layer that can still ask.

Make engagement a mandatory deliverable, not a stance. Add a required '## Questions Asked & Answered' section to the idea-decisions.md template (each consequential decision: the question put to the user + their answer, or 'user explicitly accepted default X'). Rule: every Priority Target (detected surface, core-noun-vs-qualifier where siblings appear, scope, success criteria) must appear here as asked-and-answered or as an explicitly user-accepted default — inferred-from-context is NOT an acceptable answer for any one-way-door surface. This turns the existing 'kickoff is evidence, not completion' instinct into a checkable artifact a rubber-stamp run cannot satisfy.

Replace the unconditional skip hatch (lines 28-29) with a floored one. Keep 'just go' allowed for LOW-RISK ideas only: 'If the user says "just go"/"skip the grill", you may shorten grill to lean depth ONLY when refine detected NO one-way-door surface (Detected Surfaces = none). If ANY such surface was detected, you must still lock its Surface Ruling before launch — that question is non-skippable; tell the user the build cannot ask later and a one-way-door surface left vague becomes an irreversible wrong assumption, not a recoverable one.' This removes the sanctioned path to the exact V1 failure while preserving lean speed for genuinely trivial ideas.

findings.notes: Why "partial" not "yes": the modules say the right things, but V1's lesson is that decent words get rationalized past — and here one file (atlas.md) literally encodes the skip as a supported option, while the grill's launch step has no precondition tied to the Surface Rulings / Open-Blocking outputs it already produces. Why "partial" not "no": refine/grill do carry real anti-skip stance ("never infer from silence", "wait", "never assume on a one-way-door surface unless explicitly accepted") and the output template already has the Surface Rulings + Open/Blocking fields — the teeth needed are small (wire those existing fields to a named BLOCKER + add an asked/answered deliverable + floor the skip hatch), not a rewrite. All three changes are lean: they convert existing prose fields into a checkable terminal state at the front door, which DESIGN §12 confirms is the only layer that can still ask the human. No engine change required, but if desired the engine could additionally refuse to start when args.decisions lacks rulings for declared surfaces — out of scope for these three files.

findings.evidence: S7's PASS gate `MAIN_SPEC_VERIFIED` (s7-verify-main-spec.md lines 125-139) names exactly four criteria, and ALL FOUR attest spec-coverage / cross-phase behavior, none attest that the disciplines were exercised: `mainspec_criteria_pass` ("every main-spec acceptance criterion is PASS ... against observable behavior"), `R4_compose` ("cross-phase integration observed working"), `tamper_audit` (scoped to "cross-phase oracles are spec-anchored" — line 136, NOT the per-phase R2/R3/grounding), and `shipSafe` (no obstruction on an unsafe interim). `failedCriterion` is locked to that enum (line 152, 214). A grep of S7 for discipline/grounded/read-before-write/thin/vertical-slice/two-hat/attest returns only *compose-references* to other modules for the cross-phase job — zero attestation that grounding (read-before-write), thin-vertical-slicing, two-hats, or per-phase browser verification actually happened. S7 explicitly *trusts* the phase verdicts: "each phase's verification.md (S6's per-phase artifacts) — what each phase already proved" (lines 16, 24-25 "The slices were verified at S5, the phases at S6"), and it deliberately does NOT re-prove them ("Do not re-audit passing slices ... re-run green phase suites", lines 38-40). So the four user-named discipline claims map as: (1) "explored codebase + FOLLOWED patterns" = read-before-write `grounded` gate — exists ONLY inside S2/S4 (read-before-write.md lines 95-110), never re-attested at the capstone; (2) "WROTE end-to-end tests and they PASS" — S7 only re-runs the *cross-phase* path (lines 88-92), never attests each phase reached its mandatory R2/R3; (3) "browser-verified in the RIGHT environment" — S7 screenshots the cross-phase UI flow (line 68) but the UI checklist (honest-verification.md lines 132-147) checks "real data not placeholder" and NEVER the *environment* (a grep for environment across all verifier steps finds only "environment cannot reach the entry point" = the blocked case, never "right/correct environment"); (4) "THIN VERTICAL SLICES" (DESIGN §1.6) — checked per-slice at S4 `every-change-maps`/`two-hats-holds` (s4-build.md lines 121-138), never at the capstone. Net: an agent that skipped grounding, shipped one fat slice, never browser-verified a phase, and committed mixed refactor+behavior — but whose END STATE superficially renders correctly — passes all four S7 criteria, because they only require the composed behavior to run. DESIGN §1.11 says this confidently-wrong class "survives every earlier gate," yet the capstone re-checks only behavior.
findings.gap: There is NO final, evidence-backed attestation that the disciplines were ACTUALLY exercised — only that the spec is satisfied and cross-phase behavior composes. Three specific holes an agent can rationalize past: (A) NO discipline-exercise gate criterion exists at all; the `MAIN_SPEC_VERIFIED` enum is closed to spec/behavior criteria, so even a verifier who *notices* a skipped discipline has no named criterion to FAIL on and (per DESIGN §1.2 "named, never prose") cannot block on prose. (B) The required evidence is artifact-of-BEHAVIOR (screenshot, plan-diff, exit-code), never artifact-of-DISCIPLINE — S7 never requires the grounding note (`WHAT EXISTS/REUSE THESE/INVARIANTS` in each build.md), the per-commit hat declarations, the per-phase R2/R3 evidence rows, or the thin-slice footprint-maps to be present and non-empty on disk. So "the disciplines were followed" is pure self-assertion / inherited trust, exactly the V1 rationalize-past pattern. (C) "RIGHT environment" is undefined and unchecked anywhere — no verifier confirms the browser/behavior verification ran against the correct build/base-URL/data-tier rather than a stale dev server, mock fixtures, or the wrong environment; a clean screenshot from the WRONG environment passes today. Because S7 is a SEPARATE agent from the S4 builder and already reads every phase's on-disk verification.md, it is structurally positioned to spot-check these artifacts — it simply is not told to, and has no criterion to fail.

hardening.mechanism: Add a FIFTH named gate criterion `disciplines_attested` to the `MAIN_SPEC_VERIFIED` PASS condition (the gate block ~lines 125-139) and to the `failedCriterion` enum (lines 152, 214-216) and the FAIL/BLOCKED routing. Define it as a mandatory, EVIDENCE-BACKED capstone attestation — each of four sub-claims PASSES only when S7 (the non-builder verifier) cites a concrete on-disk artifact it inspected itself, NOT a phase verdict it inherited; a sub-claim that can only be backed by self-assertion or a downstream tick is a FAIL. The four sub-claims, each with its required artifact: (1) GROUNDED-AND-REUSED — every phase's slices left a non-empty read-before-write grounding note in build.md (`WHAT EXISTS`/`REUSE THESE`/`INVARIANTS` populated, not 'none' by skipping); S7 spot-checks ≥1 slice per phase and confirms the named real entry point matches the one S7 itself exercised in R4_compose, and that no duplicate of a `REUSE THESE` helper was written. Backing artifact: the grounding note + a grep showing the reused symbol is called, not reimplemented. (2) TESTS-WRITTEN-AND-PASS — each phase's verification.md shows its mandatory rung (R2 for STANDARD, R3 for CRITICAL) was actually REACHED with a cited evidence artifact per criterion (not CODE-VERIFIED); S7 confirms the per-criterion evidence column is an observation+artifact, not 'looks correct'. Backing artifact: the phase per-criterion table evidence cells. (3) BROWSER-VERIFIED-RIGHT-ENV — for every UI/observable surface fired, an actually-captured screenshot/sample-diff/plan-diff/exit-code exists on disk AND records the ENVIRONMENT it ran against (base URL / build SHA / data tier / simulator target), and that environment is the spec-intended one (real data, real build — not a stale dev server, mock fixtures, or wrong tier). S7 re-runs the cross-phase flow in that same environment for R4_compose. A screenshot with no recorded environment, or from the wrong environment, FAILS this sub-claim (and is a tamper per honest-verification UI). Backing artifact: the screenshot file + its recorded env stamp. (4) THIN-SLICES-AND-TWO-HATS — the on-disk slices/ set shows behavior built in thin vertical slices (each slice one named behavior, `every-change-maps` held) with two-hats commits (refactor XOR behavior), per S5's recorded two-hats result; S7 confirms no phase was delivered as one fat slice that pierced no layer or mixed structure+behavior. Backing artifact: the slices/ dir + each build-verification.md two-hats line. State plainly: `disciplines_attested` FAILS (→ routeBack: S4 for a build-discipline gap that a re-build can cure, e.g. missing grounding/fat-slice; S1 if thin-slicing was impossible as phased) or BLOCKED (→ degrade) the moment any sub-claim lacks its backing artifact — 'the phase verdict said so' is explicitly NOT acceptable backing. Add a row block to the verification.md artifact template (~lines 164-189): a `## Disciplines attested` table, one row per sub-claim → PASS/FAIL + the exact artifact path/grep S7 inspected. Keep it lean: four rows, each an artifact citation, no prose ceremony — teeth, not a checklist to skim.

Because S7 FOLDS INTO S6 for single-phase initiatives (S7 lines 9-11; S6 lines 24-26, 136-153 Section 4), the same capstone attestation must fire there or single-phase builds escape it entirely. In Section 4 (the single-phase delta, ~lines 136-153) add `disciplines_attested` as a named criterion alongside `main_spec_covered` and `ship_safe`, with the identical four evidence-backed sub-claims (grounded-and-reused / tests-written-and-pass / browser-verified-right-env / thin-slices-and-two-hats), each backed by an on-disk artifact S6 inspects itself — and add it to the gate list (clause 7, ~line 200) and the `failedCriterion` enum. Make the single-phase 'right environment' check explicit since S6 owns the final ship verdict here: the integrated-flow screenshot (criterion 3 `slices_compose`, lines 80-83) must record and verify the environment it ran in, not just 'real data'. Keep it to the same four-row artifact-citation table written into the phase verification.md.

findings.notes: Severity: high — this is the capstone the user explicitly wants and it is genuinely absent, not merely weak. S7 today is a strong CROSS-PHASE SPEC/BEHAVIOR gate (its actual designed job) but it conflates "the composed system behaves to spec" with "the disciplines that make the system trustworthy were exercised." Those are different failure classes: a superficially-working end state can be produced by skipping grounding (duplication/missed-layer bugs lurking), shipping fat slices (untestable, un-revertible), never browser-verifying phases (the cross-phase screenshot can pass while an intermediate phase's UI is broken in a path the final flow doesn't traverse), or verifying in the wrong environment (green against mock/stale data). The fix is genuinely lean and anti-rationalization because every required artifact ALREADY EXISTS on disk (grounding notes in build.md, hats + footprint-maps in build.md/build-verification.md, screenshots + R2/R3 evidence rows in build-verification.md, ARCH-DEBT/ADRs) — S7 is already a non-builder agent that already reads phase verification.md, so this adds a spot-check + a named criterion + a four-row citation table, not a new ceremony. Two design choices worth flagging for the implementer: (1) the attestation must be EVIDENCE-BACKED (cite the artifact S7 inspected), never inherited-trust ('phase verdict said so'), or it reduces to the exact V1 rationalize-past pattern; (2) it must be a NAMED enum criterion (DESIGN §1.2) so a verifier who spots a skipped discipline has teeth to block — prose findings are unroutable. 'Right environment' is the single thing with NO existing coverage anywhere (confirmed by grep) and needs a new evidence requirement: the verification artifact must record the environment (base URL / build SHA / data tier / simulator target) it ran against, and S7 must confirm it is the spec-intended one. Files inspected to reach this: s7-verify-main-spec.md, s6-verify-phase.md, honest-verification.md, DESIGN.md, read-before-write.md, s5-verify-build.md, s4-build.md (all under /Users/cracklehat/Sites/workflow-exploration/atlas-engine/).

findings.evidence:
v2 covers ONE of the two V1 escape hatches and misses the other entirely.

COVERED (the "claim done when R2 is blocked" hatch): honest-verification.md lines 57-76 force the `verified` vs `code-verified` split, and crucially line 73-75: "code-verified on a STANDARD/CRITICAL slice is NOT verdict:PASS. Emit verdict:BLOCKED ... never silently downgrade verified to a green tick." This genuinely stops "R2 blocked → tick green anyway." DESIGN §1.3 ("Green tests... never reported as verified") and §1.11 back it. Good teeth here.

NOT COVERED (the "silently route AROUND the obstacle onto a convenient substitute, then claim a real pass" hatch — which is the actual V1 meta-pattern):

1. The obstruction-loop Channel A triggers (obstruction-loop.md lines 20-26 / DESIGN §7) are FOUR architectural-scope facts only: editing outside footprint, touching a shared foundation, a new persistent schema/contract/API not in plan, competing hard-to-reverse designs. A *blocked prerequisite, missing env var, unavailable/failed tool, "no data", or an already-planned-but-broken dependency/migration* is NOT in that trigger list. So an agent that hits "no seed data" or "Stripe key missing" or "tool auth failed" has NO mandate to declare an Obstruction — it falls straight through the loop. The loop is built for "architecture fights EXPANSION," not "a prerequisite is MISSING." That is precisely the class V1 weaponized.

2. honest-verification.md forbids claiming `verified` when R2 is *blocked*, but defines NO criterion forbidding a *manufactured* R2 on a SUBSTITUTE. R2's PASS criterion (line 25) is "real entry point with real data" — but "real" is asserted by the same ladder narrative; there is no named gate clause "the entry point/datastore/dataset/tool you exercised must be the slice's actual production target, not a swapped-in substitute (test DB, seeded fake rows, mocked tool, alternate endpoint) introduced to get green." An agent that switches to a test DB and runs the behavior there genuinely *reaches* R2 and reports `verified`. The tamper audit (lines 91-110) targets test-level gaming (always-true asserts, mocks, weakened assertions) — it does NOT name "ran the real behavior against a substituted environment/data/tool to dodge the blocker."

3. s4-build.md gate (lines 121-137) criteria are compiles-and-runs, every-change-maps, two-hats, guardrails-attached, obstruction-resolved. NONE names "a required prereq was silently substituted, stubbed-to-pass, or dropped." Worse, `every-change-maps` (line 126) cuts the WRONG way: seeding fake data / swapping to a test DB / deleting the failing tool maps cleanly to "make criterion X green," so the workaround PASSES every-change-maps. The one honest exit — reversibleInterim (Tier 3) — is flag-gated/default-OFF and must NOT walk the door; a silent workaround is the opposite (default-ON substitute presented as the real thing).

Net: V1 had "decent words" about real data; v2 inherits that and rationalizes past it the same way, because no NAMED criterion in any of the three modules makes "I hit an obstacle and substituted/skipped/dropped to get green" a hard FAIL/BLOCKED that a separate verifier checks.
findings.gap:
There is no single cross-cutting "no silent workaround" rule with teeth. Specifically missing:

(a) An OBSTACLE-CLASS trigger in the obstruction loop. The four Channel-A triggers are scope-expansion facts; a MISSING/BROKEN/UNAVAILABLE prerequisite (env var, secret, dependency, migration, tool/integration, required data) is not a declared-obstruction trigger, so it is never forced into the fix-or-BLOCK funnel.

(b) A named BUILD-GATE criterion that an agent cannot rationalize past: every obstacle was either genuinely fixed OR surfaced as a hard blocker — never substituted (test DB for prod DB, seeded/fake data for real data, mock/alternate endpoint for the real entry point, dropped tool) to claim a pass on a convenient stand-in.

(c) A named VERIFIER (S5/S6/S7) check — separate agent from the builder — that R2's "real entry point with real data" was the slice's ACTUAL production target, with the obstacle ledger reconciled: any obstacle the builder hit must appear as fixed (evidence) or as a BLOCKER; a green slice with an unreconciled obstacle, or one whose R2 ran against a substitute, is a tamper → blocks PASS.

The autonomous-safe shape: an unfixable obstacle must degrade the gate to BLOCKED (failedCriterion names it) so the run stops and the human sees exactly the missing prereq, then fixes + re-launches — OR, where it should have been locked before launch, the gap is named so grill front-loads it next time. Never a silent green on a substitute.

hardening.mechanism: Add the cross-cutting principle + a named, verifier-owned gate criterion `no_substitution` (the home of the no-silent-workaround rule, since this discipline already owns 'what verified is allowed to mean' and is composed by every verifier S3/S5/S6/S7). Wording gist: 'An obstacle (missing prereq/env/secret, broken or unavailable dependency, failing tool/integration, missing or unusable data, broken migration) is NEVER a license to substitute or skip. R2 is satisfied ONLY when the behavior ran through the slice's ACTUAL production entry point against its REAL data/datastore/tool — NOT a substitute introduced to get green: no swapping prod DB→test DB, no seeding fake rows to stand in for real data, no mock/alternate endpoint replacing the real entry point, no dropping a required tool/integration. Exercising the behavior against ANY such substitute is `verified`-tampering — report it like a claimed-but-unreached rung: it is `code-verified` at best and, for STANDARD/CRITICAL, BLOCKED.' Add an OBSTACLE LEDGER deliverable: the verifier must reconcile every obstacle the builder recorded — each must be (i) genuinely fixed with evidence, or (ii) carried as a BLOCKER; a PASS with an unreconciled or silently-routed-around obstacle is a `no_substitution` FAIL. Extend the tamper checklist with one bullet: '[ ] Workaround-to-green — the blocker was routed around (substitute DB/data/tool/endpoint, stub returning a canned pass, dropped requirement) and the slice reports verified on the stand-in.' Wire it into the Gate line (currently 'iff every criterion PASS AND ladderReached≥mandatory AND tamper_audit PASS...') by adding 'AND `no_substitution` PASS', and make it BLOCKED (not soft FAIL) when the obstacle is genuinely unfixable in-run so the engine degrades and the human sees the named missing prereq. Because the auditor 'MUST be a party that is NOT the implementer' (line 82) is already stated, this check is structurally enforced by the separate S5/S6/S7 verifier — the builder cannot self-certify it away.

Add a FIFTH Channel-A declared trigger so a missing/broken prereq is forced into fix-or-BLOCK instead of falling through the scope-only triggers. Wording gist (insert as item 5 in §1 Channel A): '5. A required PREREQUISITE is missing, broken, or unavailable — an env var/secret, an external dependency or service, a tool/integration, required seed/fixture data, or a migration that will not apply cleanly. This is a surface fact (the named prereq + the error), engine-corroborable.' Route it through the decision tree: if the builder can genuinely fix it inside the footprint without walking a one-way door (e.g. add the missing fixture the spec calls for) → fix and proceed; otherwise it is Tier 3 by default — you may NOT substitute or drop it. The only legal holding move is the `reversibleInterim` (flag-gated, default-OFF, app-runnable) PLUS a BLOCKER, and — critically — the slice CANNOT report R2 `verified` on the interim (cross-ref honest-verification `no_substitution`). If the missing prereq is something that should have been locked before launch (a secret/decision/data source the autonomous run cannot obtain), emit a `routeBack` so the run degrades with the prereq NAMED, so the human supplies it and re-launches (and grill front-loads it next time). State explicitly: 'unavailable prereq is one-way by default — substituting a stand-in to get green is the silent-workaround failure this trigger exists to catch; treat it as one-way unless the fix is provably in-footprint and behavior-real.'

Make the builder DECLARE obstacles rather than silently absorb them, and add the matching gate criterion so the separate verifier can catch a substitution. In §3/§5: 'If a required prereq is missing/broken/unavailable (env, secret, dependency, tool, data, migration), you MUST declare it via obstruction-loop Channel-A trigger 5 — fix it for real, or surface it as a BLOCKER/reversibleInterim. You may NOT substitute (test DB for prod, seeded/fake data for real, mock/alternate endpoint, dropped tool) or skip the slice's real path to manufacture a green build. Record every obstacle hit in build.md's obstacle ledger: prereq → fixed-with-evidence | BLOCKED.' Add gate criterion 6 `no-silent-workaround`: 'every obstacle the build hit is either fixed (evidence in build.md) or surfaced as a declared Obstruction/blocker; no required DB/data/tool/endpoint was substituted or dropped to pass. A substituted-or-skipped prereq is `built:FAIL no-silent-workaround` (→ routeBack to S2 if the plan never provided the prereq, or BLOCKED if unobtainable in-run).' Note explicitly that `every-change-maps` does NOT cover this (a workaround maps cleanly to 'make the criterion green'), and add the obstacle ledger to the build.md artifact list so the separate S5 verifier has the evidence to run `no_substitution` against.

findings.notes: Brutally-honest read: v2 is genuinely strong on HALF of this failure — the "R2 is blocked, tick green anyway" hatch is closed hard by the verified/code-verified→BLOCKED rule. But the actual V1 meta-pattern was the OTHER half: route AROUND the obstacle onto a convenient substitute and claim a *real* pass. That half is effectively uncovered: the obstruction loop only triggers on architectural scope-expansion, not on a missing/broken prerequisite; honest-verification forbids claiming verified when R2 is *blocked* but never forbids a *manufactured* R2 on a substitute datastore/data/tool/endpoint; and the s4 gate's `every-change-maps` actively rewards the workaround because the stand-in maps cleanly to the green criterion. The fix is one cross-cutting principle ('fix it or BLOCK it — never substitute, skip, or verify-elsewhere') with three coordinated teeth: a new Channel-A obstruction trigger (force missing-prereq into the funnel), a verifier-owned `no_substitution` criterion + obstacle-ledger reconciliation (catch the substitute at the separate-verifier gate, autonomous-safe via BLOCKED→degrade), and an s4 `no-silent-workaround` build criterion + ledger deliverable (force the builder to declare, not absorb). All three are named criteria a separate agent checks against on-disk evidence — not prose an in-sunk-cost builder can rationalize past — and the unfixable case degrades to a BLOCKED gate that surfaces the exact missing prereq to the human, which is the only autonomous-safe substitute for 'just ask the user.'

plan.principle: A verifier may credit ONLY behavior it observed running through the slice's REAL, DESIGNATED production path — its true entry point, real data, real datastore/tool, in the environment the human actually uses. Every prerequisite, mechanism, or sibling that path requires (env vars, secrets, tools/auth, the migration CLI, the existing house pattern, the dev DB) is EITHER front-loaded at the front door before the hands-off launch OR surfaced as a NAMED hard blocker that degrades the run and tells the human exactly what to provision and re-launch with. It is NEVER silently substituted, skipped, reinvented, applied out-of-band, or attested by inherited trust. \"Green on a stand-in\" — a test DB, a fake key, a mock endpoint, a hand-rolled reimplementation, a hand-applied DDL, a phase verdict taken on faith — is a tamper, not a pass. The single autonomous-safe response to any missing/broken/unavailable prerequisite is fix-it-real or BLOCK-and-name-it; never verify-elsewhere-and-call-it-done.
