plan.finalAttestation: Add a fifth NAMED gate criterion `disciplines_attested` to S7's `MAIN_SPEC_VERIFIED` PASS condition (steps/s7-verify-main-spec.md, gate block ~lines 125-139, the `failedCriterion` enum at lines 152 and 214-216, and the FAIL/BLOCKED routing) AND mirror it into S6's single-phase delta (steps/s6-verify-phase.md Section 4 ~lines 136-153 and the gate clause ~line 200) so single-phase initiatives — which fold S7 into S6 — cannot escape it. `disciplines_attested` is the capstone evidence gate: each of FOUR sub-claims PASSES only when the verifier (a non-builder) cites a CONCRETE on-disk artifact it inspected itself; a sub-claim backed only by self-assertion or an inherited phase verdict ('the phase verdict said so') is an automatic FAIL. The four claims and their REQUIRED evidence: (1) GROUNDED-AND-REUSED — every phase's build.md grounding note has populated WHAT EXISTS / REUSE THESE / SIBLING lines (not blanked by skipping); verifier spot-checks >=1 slice/phase, confirms the named real entry point matches the one it itself drove for R4_compose, and greps that each REUSE/SIBLING symbol is actually called, not reimplemented. Evidence: the grounding note + the grep. (2) TESTS-WRITTEN-AND-PASS — each phase's build-verification.md shows its mandatory rung (R2 STANDARD / R3 CRITICAL) was REACHED with a per-criterion evidence cell that is an observation+artifact (not 'looks correct', not code-verified), AND a persistent real-entry-point behavior test exists in changedFiles for each STANDARD+ slice and currently passes. Evidence: the per-criterion evidence cells + the committed test file paths. (3) BROWSER-VERIFIED-RIGHT-ENV — for every UI/observable surface fired, an actually-captured screenshot/sample-diff/exit-code exists on disk AND records the ENVIRONMENT it ran against (base URL / build SHA / data tier / simulator target), and that environment is the spec-intended designated one (real data, real build — not a stale dev server, mock fixtures, or wrong tier); verifier re-runs the cross-phase flow in that same environment. A screenshot with no recorded environment, or from the wrong environment, FAILS (and is a tamper). Evidence: the artifact file + its env stamp. (4) THIN-SLICES-AND-TWO-HATS — the on-disk slices/ set shows each slice is one named behavior with every-change-maps held and two-hats commits (refactor XOR behavior, per S5's recorded result); no phase shipped as one fat mixed slice. Evidence: the slices/ dir + each build-verification.md two-hats line. Routing: `disciplines_attested` FAILS to routeBack:S4 (a build-discipline gap a re-build can cure — missing grounding, fat slice, missing behavior test) or S1 (thin-slicing was impossible as phased), or BLOCKED (degrade) the moment any sub-claim lacks its backing artifact. Lean: a four-row '## Disciplines attested' table in the verification.md template (s7 ~lines 164-189, s6 artifact), one row per claim -> PASS/FAIL + the exact artifact path/grep inspected — teeth, no prose ceremony. This is the only place 'the disciplines were actually exercised' (vs 'the spec composes') is checked, and it converts inherited trust — the exact V1 rationalize-past pattern — into evidence the non-builder verifier must cite.

Make the single-phase 'right environment' check explicit: the integrated-flow screenshot in criterion 3 `slices_compose` (lines 66-83) must RECORD and VERIFY the environment it ran in (base URL / build SHA / data tier / simulator target), not just 'real data not placeholder'.
edits.why: Closes the single-phase escape hatch in final-attestation: S7 folds into S6 for single-phase initiatives (s7 lines 9-11, s6 lines 24-26, 136-153), so without this mirror every single-phase build — the common case — escapes the entire discipline-attestation gate and the right-environment check.

edits.change: Add THREE wired teeth at the ONLY layer that can still ask the human (front-load OR surface-as-blocker; never ask mid-run). (a) NEW mandatory Priority Target 'Required build capabilities & runtime config (RUN-AND-VERIFY prerequisites)': for every detected surface and integration, enumerate every external thing the AUTONOMOUS build must already have to do the real work AND to verify R2 — CLIs/tools (neon/psql/terraform), credentials & auth, accounts/project access, network/service reachability, env vars / secrets / connection strings, sandbox-or-test credentials, AND the DESIGNATED verification environment (the dev DB/branch the human uses). For EACH: what it is, why the build needs it, how the human provisions it, and a value-or-fake disposition marked provide-now | local-fake-OK | sandbox-key-here | none-needed. (b) NEW required idea-decisions.md sections: '## Required Capabilities & Preflight' (capability | why needed | provisioned? yes/waived-with-fallback | how to verify present), '## Designated Environment' (the dev DB/branch/data-tier R2 pins to), and '## Questions Asked & Answered' (each consequential decision: question put to the user + their answer, or 'user explicitly accepted default X' — inferred-from-context is NOT acceptable for any one-way-door surface). (c) NEW launch args: requiredCapabilities:<table or none>, requiredConfig:<name|purpose|how-to-obtain|value-or-fake-or-deferred> added to the Build Launch Args block (lines 74-83). Add the named SURFACE-LOCK + PREFLIGHT launch-blocking gate, tied to the existing '## Open / Blocking' line as the machine-readable verdict: 'BLOCKER — do NOT call the Workflow tool if (i) any surface detected by refine lacks an explicit Surface Ruling or user-accepted assumption, OR (ii) any required capability/config on a domain/external surface is un-provisioned AND un-waived. An empty/placeholder ## Surface Rulings with a non-none Detected Surfaces list = BLOCKED, not launchable. On BLOCKED, present the unresolved item as the blocking question and wait.' State verbatim: 'a needed tool/credential/env-var the build discovers missing mid-run is an un-fixable hard blocker — front-load it or you have shipped a guaranteed degraded run.'
edits.why: Closes the FRONT-LOAD halves left fully open: env-var-excuse limb A (the human is NEVER prompted to provide the env var before launch — no front-door deliverable enumerated required runtime config), tool-auth-gaveup front-load (neon-CLI-not-authenticated is never anticipated, only discoverable mid-run when nobody can be asked), wrong-verify-environment (no source-of-truth for 'the designated env' — teeth in S5 fall back to repo default without it), and front-door-skipped holes 2+3 (Surface Rulings/Open-Blocking outputs existed but were never wired to a launch-blocking rule; engagement was a stance, not a checkable asked/answered deliverable; S0 by contract will not re-litigate). The grill is the ONLY interactive layer (DESIGN §12), so the floor must be a launch BLOCKER here, not downstream prose.

edits.change: Replace the unconditional 'just go' skip hatch (lines 28-29) with a floored one: 'If the user says "just go"/"skip the grill", you may shorten grill to lean depth ONLY when refine detected NO one-way-door surface (Detected Surfaces = none) AND no required capability/config is un-provisioned. If ANY one-way-door surface (money|auth|data|migrations|external|PII|destructive) was detected, you must still lock its Surface Ruling AND confirm its required capabilities/config before launch — that question is non-skippable. Tell the user the build cannot ask later and a one-way-door surface left vague becomes an irreversible wrong assumption, not a recoverable one.'
edits.why: Closes front-door-skipped hole 1: atlas.md literally encoded the exact V1 behavior (barrel ahead, ask nothing) as a SANCTIONED escape hatch with only a soft warning and no floor even for money/auth/destructive surfaces. Preserves lean speed for genuinely trivial ideas while removing the sanctioned path to the failure.

edits.change: Add a 5th hard question + promote it into the `grounded` gate (lines 96-106) — DEDUPED home of the 'explore how WE do it first' spine (reinvented-existing-pattern + out-of-band-manual-fix front-load): 'Q5 SIBLING-CAPABILITY & CANONICAL MECHANISM — does an equivalent capability or a canonical process-tool already exist?' MANDATORY whenever the slice introduces a capability the repo has no precedent for BY DOMAIN (new ingest/upload/export/notification/integration shape), INDEPENDENT of whether a domain surface fired. Require a by-CAPABILITY search (grep the WHOLE feature's action verbs + resource nouns — upload/ingest/import/parse/store — not just helper intents) and a written verdict line: `SIBLING: <path:symbol of the existing equivalent + its house pattern in one line> | none after capability search of <verbs/nouns greped>`. When a fired surface is managed by a canonical PROCESS/TOOL (migrations -> the migration CLI + migrations dir + version table; deps -> package manager + lockfile; generated code -> codegen config + output), require a line: `CANONICAL MECHANISM: <tool> — changes go through it via <command>; never hand-edit <tracked state>`. Add named gate criterion `sibling-surveyed` to `grounded`: FAILs as `grounded:FAIL sibling-surveyed` if the capability-level search was not performed, its verdict line is absent, or a process-managed surface fired without its mechanism located. 'none' is assertable ONLY by listing the exact verbs/nouns greped — an unlisted search is not a search.
edits.why: Closes the front-load half of reinvented-existing-pattern (the hunt was surface-triggered + helper-shaped, but the V1 failure is a FEATURE-shaped sibling that fires no domain surface — a novel-to-repo data-ingest capability never triggered the search) and out-of-band-manual-fix (the canonical migration TOOL was never a named, gated requirement, so 'go through the tool' was never front-loaded before the first edit). Makes 'go explore how WE do it before making up your own way' a checkable grounding criterion.

edits.change: Make 'reinvented an existing equivalent' an explicit named FAIL, not 'prefer reuse.' In step 6 + the `reuse-checked` gate criterion (lines 91-98, 199-200) add sub-criterion `follow-the-house-pattern`: when grounding's SIBLING line names an existing equivalent capability, the plan MUST either (a) reuse/extend it, or (b) carry a written one-line argument for why the sibling's pattern genuinely does not fit (a distinct-requirement argument). Inventing a parallel new way when a sibling exists with NO such argument is `reuse-checked:FAIL follow-the-house-pattern` — a hard FAIL that blocks readyToBuild/readyToVerify, routeBack=null (fix re-plans onto the sibling). Add a plan.md line to the Build plan section: `SIBLING PATTERN: follow <path> | divergence justified: <one-line argument>`.
edits.why: Closes reinvented-existing-pattern escape #1 (NO NAMED FAIL — 'invented a new way when an equivalent sibling exists' was nowhere a gate criterion; the closest said 'prefer/reuse', which DESIGN §1.2 says is unroutable prose) and escape #3 partially (reuse-checked was conditioned on 'what grounding FOUND' and passed vacuously on a missed sibling). The V1 user demand maps to a checkable, hard-blocking criterion.

edits.change: Add a NEW named clause `0. Through-the-tool, never out-of-band` to the migrations guardrail check (and the Pack gate FAIL list), marked oneWayDoor:true: 'The schema change is authored AS a migration via the repo's actual migration mechanism (Prisma migrate, Rails db:migrate, Alembic revision+upgrade, Flyway/Liquibase, Knex/TypeORM/Sequelize, Django makemigrations) and is a NEW TRACKED FILE the tool generated/recognizes. Hand-applying DDL (psql/mysql/console ALTER/CREATE/DROP), editing an already-applied migration in place, or hand-editing the tool's version state (schema_migrations / _prisma_migrations / alembic_version table, the migrations lockfile, generated client/schema) is a FAIL named `migration: out-of-band schema change (bypassed <tool>)`. Generalizes via the canonical guardrail to other process-managed systems — codegen output and lockfile-managed deps go through their tool, never hand-edited.' Add a verification clause `left-designated-green` to 'Migrations in verification' (lines 183-186): after the migration, the DESIGNATED dev DB/branch must be confirmed RUNNABLE and the behavior verified ON IT; verifying against the test/CI DB while the designated DB stays broken is a named FAIL `migration: broke designated DB`.
edits.why: Closes out-of-band-manual-fix (v2 had strong words on migration SHAPE/expand-contract but ZERO teeth on migration MECHANISM — the exact axis V1 violated; an agent can run perfectly-shaped DDL by hand and pass every existing check) and reinforces wrong-verify-environment on the migration path (the migration-broke-the-dev-DB-then-verified-elsewhere case).

edits.change: Add a 5th Channel-A declared-obstruction trigger (§1, after item 4) — DEDUPED home of the 'missing prerequisite / out-of-band move is an obstruction' spine: '5. A required PREREQUISITE is missing/broken/unavailable, OR a PROCESS-MANAGED system must be changed outside its tool — an env var/secret, external dependency or service, tool/integration, required seed/fixture data, a migration that will not apply cleanly; OR the only path forward hand-applies DDL/SQL, edits applied/generated migrations, hand-edits a lockfile, or edits codegen output instead of going through the canonical command. This is a surface fact (the named prereq/mechanism + the error), engine-corroborable.' Route it: a missing prereq the build can genuinely fix in-footprint -> fix; otherwise Tier 3 by default (one-way by rule — substituting a stand-in or hand-mutating tracked state is the silent-workaround/state-corruption this trigger catches), with a routeBack when the prereq should have been front-loaded (so the run degrades with the prereq NAMED and grill front-loads it next time). State: 'an out-of-band manual change to a tracked-state mechanism is a one-way door even when it feels undoable, because it silently breaks the mechanism for later; an unavailable prereq substituted to get green is the no-silent-workaround failure.'
edits.why: Closes the shared gap in no-silent-workarounds + tool-auth-gaveup + out-of-band-manual-fix: the four existing Channel-A triggers are all CODE-SHAPE/scope-expansion facts (footprint/shared-foundation/new-schema-contract-API/competing-designs); a missing env var/tool/auth or an out-of-band manual change matches NONE, so the move that 'feels like a quick fix' tripped no trigger and fell straight through the loop. P2 because the P0 S4/S5 gates already block the outcome; this generalizes the obstruction funnel and stops the rationalization at its source.

openQuestions: Engine-schema confirmation: the new launch args requiredCapabilities/requiredConfig and the S4 result field newRequiredConfig[] / obstacle ledger may need the atlas.mjs envelope + StructuredOutput schemas updated. The findings assert failedCriterion/verdict/changedFiles already exist and these are prose+gate additions, but newRequiredConfig[] and an obstacle-ledger field on the S4 envelope, plus requiredCapabilities/requiredConfig on the Workflow launch args, are genuinely new fields — confirm whether the engine reads them or whether they must live inside build.md/idea-decisions.md prose only.

Designated-environment source of truth: S5 designated-env-green and honest-verification's R2 pin read 'the dev DB/branch named in build args repoContext/constraints, else repo default'. If the grill '## Designated Environment' deliverable is skipped on a lean run, the teeth fall back to repo default (weaker). Decide whether designated-env is MANDATORY in the grill for any persistence/migration surface, or acceptably defaulted.

behavior-test-committed scope boundary: mandating a persistent real-entry-point test for every STANDARD+ slice could add weight to slices whose real entry point is genuinely un-harnessable in-repo (the legitimate code-verified case). Confirm the interaction: does a slice that is honestly external-genuinely-unavailable at R2 still owe a committed behavior test that is .skip-with-reason, or is it exempt — and does that exemption reopen the never-written-test hole?

S5 no-reinvention lens (reinvented-existing-pattern hardening #3) was proposed for s5-verify-build.md to put the capability-level sibling re-hunt on the universal verifier (closing the FAST-lane self-cert hole), but I folded reinvention enforcement into S2 follow-the-house-pattern + read-before-write sibling-surveyed + the S7 grounded-and-reused attestation to keep the plan lean. Confirm whether a dedicated S5 no-reinvention lens is still wanted for FAST-lane slices that never reach S2's full plan-verify, or whether S7 attestation is sufficient coverage.

issues.problem: INERT FIELDS — the engine does not read them. atlas.mjs lines 48-53 ingest ONLY a.initiativeId/initiativeName/idea/decisions/constraints/repoContext into the initiative object, and the step prompt (lines 185-188) forwards only idea/decisions/constraints/repoContext. New launch args requiredCapabilities/requiredConfig are silently dropped. Likewise SCHEMA_S4 (lines 136-139) has no newRequiredConfig field and the open ENVELOPE (118-124) does not carry it, so 'emit it as newRequiredConfig[] in the result' is discarded by the engine. The edits instruct agents to produce structured fields nothing consumes — the exact dead-vestige the contract bans (DESIGN §11: 'no dead vestiges').
issues.fix: Drop the launch-arg + return-field framing. Route both through the ONE channel the engine actually reads: (a) grill writes '## Required Capabilities & Preflight' + '## Designated Environment' into idea-decisions.md (S0 reads that file verbatim at engine line 424 and it 'TAKES PRECEDENCE over the args summary'); (b) S4 records new runtime config in build.md's obstacle ledger / grounding note as on-disk prose the S5 verifier reconciles. Delete every 'add launch arg' and 'emit newRequiredConfig[] in the result' clause UNLESS the plan also commits to patching atlas.mjs (ingest + ENVELOPE/SCHEMA_S4) — and openQuestion #1 should be resolved as 'prose-on-disk only, no engine change' since that is the lean autonomous-safe answer.

issues.about: disciplines_attested (s7 + s6) layered on top of s5's three new sub-criteria + s6's existing 5 + s4's three new
issues.problem: OVER-ENGINEERING / ceremony-on-everything — the failure the whole design fights (DESIGN §1.8: heavy guardrails train agents to skim the checklist that guards the money path). disciplines_attested adds FOUR artifact-cited sub-claims, each mandating a grep or file-path citation, MIRRORED into both S6 and S7. For the COMMON single-phase case S6 now runs: 5 phase criteria + tamper audit + main_spec_covered + ship_safe + a 4-row disciplines table + the env-stamp check on slices_compose. Sub-claims (1) GROUNDED-AND-REUSED, (2) TESTS-WRITTEN-AND-PASS, (3) BROWSER-VERIFIED-RIGHT-ENV, (4) THIN-SLICES re-derive what S2 grounded, S5 verified (behavior-test-present, designated-env-green), and S5 two-hats already gated per slice — S7/S6 re-attesting them is duplicate verification, not a new check, for everything except the genuinely-new 'were the disciplines exercised at all' meta-question.
issues.fix: Keep disciplines_attested but CUT it to the one thing genuinely absent: a spot-check that the disciplines were EXERCISED (not a re-verification of each). One sub-claim, not four: 'spot-check >=1 slice/phase — its build.md grounding note is populated (not blanked), its build-verification.md reached its mandatory rung with a committed behavior test, and (if a UI/observable surface fired anywhere) >=1 env-stamped screenshot exists. If any is absent -> FAIL routeBack:S4.' This catches the skipped-grounding / fat-slice / never-browser-verified class WITHOUT re-walking four artifact citations per claim across two mirrored gates. Claims (1)(2)(4) are already each a named S5 per-slice gate after these edits; S7 re-citing them is the ceremony. Keep claim (3)'s env-stamp ONLY because right-environment has no per-slice home until s5 designated-env-green lands — fold it into the single spot-check.

issues.about: s5-verify-build.md behavior-test-present FAIL routeBack:null, and s4 behavior-test-committed
issues.problem: Potential AUTONOMOUS-SAFETY conflict with the legitimate code-verified path. behavior-test-present mandates a persistent real-entry-point test that PASSES for every STANDARD+ slice, FAIL routeBack:null (S4 re-builds). But honest-verification's own code-verified contract (lines 62-75) exists precisely for slices whose real entry point is genuinely un-harnessable (external-genuinely-unavailable). openQuestion #3 flags this and it is NOT resolved in the edits: a slice that is honestly external-genuinely-unavailable at R2 cannot write a PASSING real-entry-point test, yet behavior-test-present demands one and routes to a re-build that cannot succeed — an infinite fix loop until maxFixAttempts blocks, degrading a slice that was correctly code-verified. The two new teeth (behavior-test-present hard-FAIL vs the preserved code-verified BLOCKED path) can fire on the same slice.
issues.fix: State the precedence explicitly in the s5 edit: behavior-test-present FAILs ONLY when R2 was REACHABLE (the no_substitution blocker-classification returned anything other than external-genuinely-unavailable). When the slice is legitimately external-genuinely-unavailable, the owed deliverable is a committed test marked skip-with-reason naming the exact blocker (so the hole cannot reopen as 'never written'), and the verdict is BLOCKED code-verified, NOT FAIL. Resolve openQuestion #3 in the edit text, not as a deferred question — an unresolved precedence between two new gates is exactly the rationalize-past ambiguity V1 exploited.

issues.about: grill SURFACE-LOCK + PREFLIGHT launch-blocking gate, and atlas.md floored skip-hatch
issues.problem: AUTONOMOUS-SAFE and correct in shape, but the BLOCKER lives in the interactive layer where the only enforcement is the conversational agent's own compliance — there is no engine-side check that a launched run actually had its surfaces locked. The grill edit says 'do NOT call the Workflow tool if...' but nothing stops S0 from running with an idea-decisions.md whose '## Surface Rulings' is empty. This is acceptable per DESIGN §12 (grill is the only interactive layer) but the plan asserts it 'closes' front-door-skipped when it only closes it AT the front door — a caller who bypasses the grill entirely (calls the Workflow tool directly) still barrels in.
issues.fix: Add a lightweight S0 floor that is NOT re-litigation (DESIGN says S0 won't re-litigate engagement, correctly): if idea-decisions.md exists AND lists Detected Surfaces != none AND '## Surface Rulings' is empty/placeholder, S0 records a blocker naming the unruled surface and degrades — the autonomous-safe 'surface it as a hard blocker so the human sees exactly what's needed and re-launches' pattern from the key constraints. This is one existence-check on a file S0 already reads (line 424), not a re-grill. Without it, 'closes front-door-skipped' overclaims — it closes the sanctioned-hatch (atlas.md) but not the direct-launch bypass.

issues.problem: Sound and well-targeted (out-of-band-manual-fix had zero MECHANISM teeth — confirmed: existing migrations guardrail covers expand/contract SHAPE only, lines 87-103, nothing on whether DDL went through the tool). But marking the through-the-tool check oneWayDoor:true is redundant: the migration surface is ALREADY oneWayDoor:true by rule (web-backend migrations guardrail header line 87, and obstruction-loop §3 'persistence migrations one-way by rule'). Re-flagging the sub-clause adds no routing change and risks the reader inferring a second independent one-way door.
issues.fix: Drop the explicit oneWayDoor:true on clause 0 — it inherits one-way from the migration surface. Keep the FAIL name 'migration: out-of-band schema change (bypassed <tool>)' and the left-designated-green verification clause; those are the real new teeth. Minor, but the leanness bar (DESIGN §11) says cut anything that doesn't change what the agent does.

overEngineering: disciplines_attested four-artifact-cited sub-claims mirrored into BOTH s6 and s7 — re-attests grounding (S2 gate), behavior-test (new S5 gate), two-hats (existing S5 gate) and env (new S5 gate) that are ALREADY per-slice named criteria after these edits. Cut to one EXERCISED-the-disciplines spot-check; the rest is duplicate verification dressed as a capstone, the exact skim-the-checklist failure DESIGN §1.8 names.

s5 gains three named sub-criteria (behavior-test-present, designated-env-green, no_substitution) on top of its existing 5-clause PASS gate + tamper audit + two-hats + R4_compose. With s4 also gaining three (no-silent-workaround, behavior-test-committed, declare-new-runtime-config) and s6/s7 the attestation table, a single STANDARD slice now passes ~a dozen named gates across S4+S5. Verify the marginal ones earn their slot or fold behavior-test-committed (S4) and behavior-test-present (S5) recognition that they are the SAME deliverable checked once at build, once at verify — state it as 'S5 confirms the S4-committed test', not two independently-derived criteria.
oneWayDoor:true on web-backend migrations clause 0 — redundant with the migration surface's existing by-rule one-way flag.

The principle paragraph and the honest-verification no_substitution wording restate the same spine at near-paragraph length in two places (the cross-cutting principle field AND the honest-verification edit). The discipline edit is the deduped home; the principle field should be one or two sentences pointing AT it, not a parallel full statement.
gapsRemaining: env-var-excuse: PARTIALLY open at runtime. The build-time half (s4 capability-unavailable BLOCKER) and verify-time half (s5 blocker-classification -> R2_blocked_missing_config) are well-closed AS PROSE GATES. But the front-load half routes through grill launch args requiredConfig that the engine never reads (atlas.mjs 50-53) — it survives only if grill writes it into idea-decisions.md AND s0 surfaces it. As written, the front-load deliverable is an inert arg, so the 'human is prompted to provide the env var before launch' guarantee depends entirely on the grill agent voluntarily writing the on-disk section. Fix the channel (idea-decisions.md, not launch args) or the front-load half stays a stance, not a wired deliverable.

front-door-skipped: closes the SANCTIONED hatch (atlas.md floored skip) and the grill internal gate, but NOT the direct-launch bypass — nothing engine-side blocks a run whose idea-decisions.md has detected surfaces with empty Surface Rulings. Needs the one-line S0 existence-check floor (see issue 4) to be genuinely closed rather than closed-only-if-the-front-door-was-used.

right-environment for the legitimate code-verified slice: designated-env-green and no_substitution close the substitute-to-get-green tamper well, but openQuestion #2 (is '## Designated Environment' mandatory in grill for persistence/migration surfaces, or defaulted?) is left unresolved. If the grill section is skipped on a lean run, s5 designated-env-green falls back to repo default — weaker exactly on the migration path where wrong-verify-environment originally bit. Decide it in the edit: mandatory for any persistence/migration/external surface, defaulted otherwise.
